titlestmt.titleproper: Donaldson (Will) collection of material by Theodore Dreiser and Dreiseriana
Finding Aid for the Will Donaldson Collection of Material by Theodore Dreiser and Dreiseriana LSC.0659
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Manuscripts Division staff; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2021 July 22.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-07-22 11:28:01 -0700.

did.unittitle: Will Donaldson collection of material by Theodore Dreiser and Dreiseriana
did.abstract: Theodore Herman Albert Dreiser (1871-1945) was an author whose publications include: Sister Carrie (1990), Jennie Gerhardt (1911), The financier (1912), The titan (1914), Twelve Men (1919), and An American Tragedy (1925). After 1925, he ventured into social commentary and political analysis. The collection contains letters, clippings, photographs, films, manuscripts, and a scrapbook. There is a collection of Dreiser's printed work including first editions, inscribed, and other significant editions.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Theodore Herman Albert Dreiser was born in Terre Haute, Indiana, on August 27, 1871; briefly attended the University of Indiana; held a succession of newspaper and magazine jobs in Chicago, St. Louis, Pittsburgh, and New York; awarded the Merit Medal for fiction by the American Academy of Arts and Letters, 1944; publications include: Sister Carrie (1900), Jennie Gerhardt (1911), The financier (1912), The titan (1914), Twelve men (1919), and An American tragedy (1925); after 1925, he ventured into social commentary and political analysis, joining the Communist Party shortly before his death; died in Hollywood, California, on December 28, 1945.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Will Donaldson Collection of Material by Theodore Dreiser and Dreiseriana (Collection 659). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection contains material related to Dreiser's career as a writer. Includes letters, clippings, photographs, films, and a scrapbook. Also contains manuscripts including his autobiography titled, A book about myself. Additionally, there is a collection of Dreiser's printed work including first editions, inscribed, and other significant editions. Also includes a small amount of material related to the career of Donaldson.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

did.physdesc: 699 leaves. 28cm. Typescript (carbon) with holograph corrections and additions throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Original title, which is corrected, was A novel about myself. Together with a letter by Dreiser to Mr. Lange to whom Dreiser sold the manuscript. What I am offering you is the only complete typewritten copy of the book with many marginal changes by me--the copy from which the book was set up. This copy contains some chapters not in the book. They were struck out by me. This letter is written from Los Angeles and dated September 22, 1922.

did.physdesc: 52 leaves, 13 leaves irregularly numbered. 28cm. Holograph. In slip cover. Irregularly paginated, pp.1-12 are mounted, 12-21 not mounted, 22-34 mounted, 35-43 not mounted, 44-52 mounted. The irregular pages at end not mounted. The mounted pages have many corrections, the unmounted pages none.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: With holograph note by Dreiser explaining that he recopied the mounted pages as the paper had begun to crumble. Published The Century Magazine, 1916.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Answer to query What makes a perfect play?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Clippings relating to the life and literary career of Theodore Dreiser. 30 cm. The main bulk of the clippings are dated 1932.

did.unittitle: American tragedy. Motion picture version. Stills, publicity material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Envelope marked Dreiseriana. Terre Haute views 1932-- pictures of the Dreiser homes and localities visited by him and described in Hoosier Holiday taken by Will Donaldson. Photo of portrait of Dreiser owned by Mencken.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 4 typescript pages with bibliographical information. At end some anecdotes regarding Dreiser's life.

did.unittitle: Book reviews, publicity, dealer's catalogues referring to Theodore Dreiser.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Publicity material relating to the Scottsboro case. Theodore Dreiser's speech on the Scottsboro case at Town Hall, Friday Evening June 5, 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Various people to Mr. Donaldson re collecting material on Dreiser.

did.unittitle: Clippings and printed material relating to the career of Wil Donaldson, collector of Dreiser.
Dreiser, Theodore. Letters to Kelly James E.,
Letter to Frank Graham Moorhead. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letterhead Delineator, stating he would be interested in articles on certain subjects.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Gift of Jo and Flo Swerling.

did.unittitle: Letter to Sam Slingsby. New York,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Referring Mr. Slingsby to passages from his autogiographical works. Gift of Jo and Flo Swerling.

did.unittitle: Letters to Carlyle West. New York,

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Mr. West lived in Toronto, Canada.

did.unittitle: In the matter of Commonwealth vs. Donald S. Friede, ... [a case regarding obscenity in Dreiser's An American Tragedy].
Commonwealth vs. Donald S. Friede. Brief for the defendant... no.13 of 1930. With this is bound: Commonwealth vs. Donald S. Friede. Suffolk,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Both these relate to the case involving An American Tragedy.

did.unittitle: Plaster cast of hand from sculpture by Edgardo Simone of Theodore Dreiser.
